name: Dry-Marinating Ducks
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Whole Sichuan peppercorns, roasted and crushed
recipeInstructions:
1. Flatten the duck's breastbone by pressing down on it.
2. Rub the dry marinade over the trimmed duck.
3. Rub the dry marinade inside the cavity of the duck.
4. Place the duck on a baking sheet covered with another flat sheet.
5. Weight down the top sheet with water to flatten the duck and release moisture. Let marinate in a cool place or refrigerator for 2 days.
6. Use the marinated duck to make Smoked Tea Duck, steam it for 2 hours to cook and render fat, then let sit at room temperature until dry.
7. Deep-fry the duck in about 4 cups of oil until crisp.
SMOKED TEA DUCK:
1 whole duck (5-6 pounds), trimmed
6 slices fresh ginger root, cut into 3-inch sections
6 scallions, cut into 3-inch pieces
FOR SMOKING:
1 cup raw long-grain rice
1 cup dark Chinese tea leaves
1/2 cup brown sugar or hickory chips
Serves 4 to 6 as an appetizer. May be smoked up to 2 days ahead of time and refrigerated until ready to serve. Allow to reach room temperature.
1. Stuff the duck cavity with ginger and scallions.
2. Steam the duck for 1 hour 15 minutes (5-pound) to 1 hour 30 minutes (6-pound) to draw out fat and cook gently, keeping it moist. Drain and remove ginger and scallions.
3. Prepare a bed of charcoal in the barbecue.
4. Place a metal pie plate with smoking ingredients on the coals.
5. Set the duck on the grate over the coals.
6. Smoke the duck for 45 to 50 minutes, turning it every 10-15 minutes to brown evenly. Check coals periodically to ensure they stay hot.
Serve with hot mustard, hoisin sauce, and chopped green onions for dipping. Rice is also served.
